calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-20-2019 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: Notice having been given and there being no appearance nor an objection filed, the Court orders case dismissed pursuant to California Rules of Court Section 3.1385. Judge: GARRETT L. WONG; Clerk: RAYMOND K. WONG; Proceedings not reported. (610)

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-12-2019 Continued To Nov-19-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Dec-10-2018 Assigned To Trial Court

COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Dec-10-2018 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Jul-16-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion And Defendant Carrol Anne Whites Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Dec-03-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
(415-421-4400) Steven Miyake for Plaintiff/ opposing party Marie Tatman .
(925-482-8950) Marcus Brown for Defendant/Moving party Carol White.

ARGUED. The Court questioned what the statute of limitations would be in an oral agreement. Mr. Miyake responded two years. Mr. Miyake argued that the statute of limitations has not run out, that the trigger of breach began in October 2014, that the letter from 2012 shows that his client was not concerned that the parties would not follow through with completing the repairs and that the duty of inquiry does not apply because of the family relationship. Mr. Brown cited that delayed discovery rule, that if plaintiff is relying on the oral agreement statute of limitations then the complaint is untimely and that it is their burden to prove why the date of October 2017 is fine.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT CARROL WHITE's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication is taken under submission. Parties to prepare a form of order. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: N. Wong, Not Reported. (D501)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion And Defendant Carrol Anne Whites Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Nov-09-2018 Continued To Nov-21-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CARROL WHITE's Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication is continued to November 21, 2018. Even though opposition was filed on October 26, 2018, an incomplete courtesy copy of opposition has been delivered to department 501 after 4:30 p.m. on November 7, 2018 in violation of SFLR 2.7B and without any explanation for the delay; and a purportedly complete copy was delivered at 9:20 a.m. on November 6, 2018. Judge Charles Haines, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported (D501)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Amend Complaint
Off Calendar Oct-02-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Steven Miyake, esq. (415-421-4400) for Plaintiff/Moving party MARIE TATMAN.
Marcus T. Brown, esq. (925-482-8950) for Defendant/opposing party Carol Ann White via Court Call.

Hearing is held. Counsel stated all discoveries has been taken, no new facts, and stipulate to keeping the original Motion for Summary Judgment hearing date, current briefing schedule, and allowing Defendant to add fraud to her answer. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF MARIE TATMAN's Motion To Amend Complaint GRANTED per stipulation of parties. Prevailing party to submit proposed order of stipulations and, including the briefing schedule, in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. (D501)
